title: New Consumer Electronics Categories Appear in Time for Christmas
author: Russ Craig, Peter Kastner
abstract: Aberdeen Hot Topic examining new consumer electronics product categories emerging for the 2003 holiday season. Covers Hand Held Entertainment's $99 ZVUE pocket video player sold through Toys R Us using SD cards; Sony UX40 Clié at $1,359 total cost for comparable video capability; Roku HD1000 HDTV media player at $499.99 by ReplayTV founder Anthony Wood; NEC methanol fuel cell laptop prototype with 5-hour battery life; and Aberdeen's conclusion that non-traditional CE companies like Dell are disrupting traditional global CE brands through aggressive pricing and innovation.
importance_rationale: Early identification of disruption in consumer electronics from startups and non-traditional players challenging established brands on price and innovation. Captured the emergence of portable video as a consumer category before smartphones made it ubiquitous.
relevance_rationale: Specific products and companies covered are largely historical curiosities. The ZVUE player and Sony Clié are obsolete. SD cards evolved massively but the specific devices are no longer relevant. Most value is as a historical snapshot of the pre-smartphone portable media landscape.
prescience_rationale: Correctly predicted disruption from non-traditional CE players; Roku evolved from the HD1000 media player into a major streaming platform worth billions. Correctly identified price disruption as a key competitive dynamic. However fuel cell laptops never materialized for consumers; Hand Held Entertainment/ZVUE did not succeed long-term; the specific disruption pattern (Dell in CE) played out differently than expected. Mixed results overall.
